evals.prompt: I need a delete button that looks dangerous — red color, clearly destructive. How do I style sgds-button for a destructive action?
evals.expected_output: Uses tone="danger" (not variant="danger" which is deprecated since v3.5.6). Shows <sgds-button tone="danger"> with optional variant combinations.
expectations: Response uses tone="danger" as the primary approach for destructive styling
Response does NOT recommend variant="danger" as the primary approach (it is deprecated since v3.5.6)
Response shows a working code example with tone="danger"

evals.prompt: My save button triggers an API call. How do I stop the user from clicking it multiple times while the request is in flight?
evals.expected_output: Uses the loading boolean attribute on sgds-button to show a spinner and block interaction during the async operation. Sets btn.loading = true before the async call and resets to false on completion.
expectations: Response uses the loading attribute/property on sgds-button to block re-clicks
Response shows setting loading = true before the async call and loading = false after
Response does NOT suggest using disabled as the primary approach for async in-progress state

evals.prompt: I have a form dialog with a Cancel and a Confirm button. Which side should the primary Confirm button go — left or right?
evals.expected_output: Primary action goes on the RIGHT in a horizontal button group, following SGDS button group convention. Cancel/secondary is on the left.
expectations: Response places the primary action (Confirm) on the right side
Response places the secondary action (Cancel) on the left side
Response shows a code example using sgds:flex sgds:gap-2 sgds:justify-end or equivalent layout with primary button last in DOM order

evals.prompt: I want a ghost-style destructive button — no background fill, but text and border in red to indicate danger. How do I create this with sgds-button?
evals.expected_output: Combines variant="ghost" (or variant="outline") with tone="danger" to get a low-emphasis destructive button.
expectations: Response combines a non-filled variant (ghost or outline) with tone="danger"
Response shows a working code example like <sgds-button variant="ghost" tone="danger"> or <sgds-button variant="outline" tone="danger">
Response does NOT recommend using only variant="danger" without tone to achieve the ghost/outline destructive look

evals.prompt: I want a button that only shows an icon with no label text. Should I use sgds-button with an icon slot, or is there something else?
evals.expected_output: Recommends <sgds-icon-button> as the correct component for icon-only buttons, rather than sgds-button with an icon slot.
expectations: Response does NOT suggest using <sgds-button> with only an icon slot as the recommended approach
Response explains why sgds-icon-button is the right choice (purpose-built for icon-only)
